title: RPYR-0015 — `genericalias` `make_parameters_from_slice` recurses into nested list/tuple args with no guard, and RustPython computes it EAGERLY at subscript, so `list[L]` with a self-referential `L` SIGSEGVs where CPython (lazy `__parameters__`) does not
one_line_repro:
L=[]
L.append(L)
list[L]   # SIGSEGV on RustPython; CPython is fine (params are lazy)
prior_art: RustPython umbrella #2796 (open) = fuzzer RUSTPY-0007a, whose second fleet EXPLICITLY names `genericalias::make_parameters_from_slice` as a native recursion → SIGSEGV site. CPython twin filed as #154275 (`_Py_make_parameters` recurses without a guard; fix PR #154277). This is the RustPython side.
found_by: recursion-guard-auditor (informed whole-tree explore — separated this from the hash class: it is a distinct raw-Rust recursion, NOT on the object.rs:695 hash dispatch)
toolkit_class: unguarded-protocol-recursion (eager parameter walk; native stack overflow)
cpython_behavior: RustPython-only for the plain subscript `list[L]`: RustPython computes __parameters__ EAGERLY at subscript time and SIGSEGVs; CPython computes it LAZILY so `list[L]` alone is fine (CPython crashes only when __parameters__ is actually forced — that is CPython #154275). So RustPython crashes on strictly more inputs.
guarded_twin: The hash/compare/repr paths are guarded by `vm.with_recursion` (see RPYR-0014); make_parameters is a separate raw Rust recursion (`make_parameters_from_slice` self-calls at genericalias.rs:329) that never passes through any of them → needs its OWN with_recursion wrap.
affected: types.GenericAlias subscription that walks nested list/tuple args (genericalias.rs make_parameters_from_slice)
typing.Union / X|Y via union.rs:236 (delegates into the same walk)
notes: `make_parameters_from_slice` collects type parameters by recursing into any arg that is a list/tuple (genericalias.rs:329 self-call) with no recursion guard. RustPython invokes it eagerly when the alias is created (`list[L]`), so a self-referential or deeply-nested list arg overflows the native stack immediately. Verified live: `L=[]; L.append(L); list[L]` -> RustPython SIGSEGV (exit 139); CPython `list[L]` is fine (lazy) and only crashes on `list[L].__parameters__` (= #154275). Also crashes acyclically: `x=[0]; {x=[x]}x3e5; list[x]`. FIX: wrap the make_parameters descent in `vm.with_recursion(...)`, as CPython PR #154277 wraps `_Py_make_parameters` in `Py_EnterRecursiveCall`. Distinct from RPYR-0014 (that is the hash *dispatch*; this is a standalone param-walk).
